description: High-throughput, asynchronous execution over large datasets. High latency acceptable. Not for real-time user requests.
CPU-bound operations requiring significant processing power (e.g., video encoding, hashing, heuristic detection).
Operations performed on cold storage or local caches without requiring active network connections or live external APIs.
Low-latency operations expecting a human to be waiting for the output in a terminal.
Continuous, long-running processes that react to physical or software events (e.g., FileSystem Watchers).
Universal CLI User Experience engine. Decouples Commands from pure Domain Didos.
Auto-migrated use case for mount_domain.
Manages physical-to-virtual path mappings (tokens) to enable cross-device synchronization and location-agnostic metadata.
Convert absolute local file paths into abstract PATHxx tokens before storing in the shared database or sending to a remote node.
Convert virtual PATHxx tokens back into local physical paths for disk I/O, playback, or reporting.
Provides low-level physical File System interaction primitives. Focuses on high-performance directory crawling and metadata extraction.
Deeply crawl a physical directory to ingest file names, sizes, timestamps, and optional hashes into a standardized Stream.
